summary: Draft candidate package for AllowRemoteDASD under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\I/O System. The clean Win25H2Clean baseline confirmed the live value, the path-aware static pass found an exact current-build ntoskrnl.exe value hit, and a naturally resolved current-build Ghidra export showed that the strongest code route opens the removable-storage policy path instead of the intended Session Manager I/O path. The tools-hardened path-aware ETW lane completed shell-safe with a real I/O burst but stayed a clean no-hit for the intended path. That keeps the lane at Class B with runtime_no_read and path-context uncertainty.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: The 96-key phase-0 existence batch recorded `system.io-allow-remote-dasd` at HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\I/O System with observed value 0.
validation_proof.notes: Phase-0 existence confirmed the intended registry path exists on the clean baseline before path-aware routing began.

setting.name: Allow Remote DASD
setting.casual_explanation: Allow Remote DASD is a low-level Session Manager I/O value that appears related to raw disk access policy.
setting.why_people_change_it: Researchers track it when studying removable storage policy overlap and low-level I/O access behavior.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: The lane now has baseline existence, an exact current-build value-name hit, a decisive current-build Ghidra route to a removable-storage policy path, and a shell-safe intended-path ETW runtime lane that stayed no-hit.
setting.professional_notes: Phase-0 existence confirmed AllowRemoteDASD on the live Win25H2Clean baseline with preview value 0 under Session Manager I/O System. The path-aware static pass over the narrowed binary set found an exact current-build ntoskrnl.exe value hit for AllowRemoteDASD, but no exact intended path-context string in the narrowed exact-string scan. The decisive current-build Ghidra export then showed the naturally resolved ntoskrnl.exe function opening \REGISTRY\MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\RemovableStorageDevices and querying AllowRemoteDASD there. A tools-hardened path-aware ETW lane then ran both short and split I/O-burst traces against the intended Session Manager I/O path on RegProbe-Baseline-ToolsHardened-20260330. It produced real ETL/CSV outputs, kept shell health clean, and still returned a clean no-hit for both the exact value name and the intended path.

allowed_values.label: Observed baseline
allowed_values.meaning: The clean Win25H2Clean baseline exposes AllowRemoteDASD=0 at the intended Session Manager I/O path.

targets.notes: The intended Session Manager I/O value exists, but the strongest current-build static route points at a removable-storage policy path rather than the intended path.

app_current_implementation.provider_source: not currently shipped in the app
app_current_implementation.notes: The current app does not expose AllowRemoteDASD as a direct tweak or UI surface.

windows_defaults.label: Observed Win25H2Clean baseline

states.rationale: Keep the observed baseline while the intended path remains research-only and unresolved.

windows_defaults.notes: Observed directly from the 96-key phase-0 existence batch on Win25H2Clean.

recommended_profiles.label: Observed baseline

states.rationale: Track the observed baseline value without turning it into a shipped preset.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the repo to track the live baseline and current evidence for this disputed low-level I/O value.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a cleanly attributed, runtime-proven control surface.
tradeoffs: Keeps the candidate research-only and non-actionable.

evidence.title: Win25H2Clean 96-key phase-0 existence batch
evidence.summary: The 96-key existence batch confirmed AllowRemoteDASD exists on the clean baseline at the intended Session Manager I/O path with observed value 0.

evidence.title: Path-aware static probe for AllowRemoteDASD
evidence.summary: The path-aware static pass found an exact current-build ntoskrnl.exe value hit for AllowRemoteDASD, but the naturally resolved current-build Ghidra function opened the removable-storage policy path rather than the intended Session Manager I/O path.

evidence.title: Path-aware lightweight ETW follow-up for AllowRemoteDASD
evidence.summary: The tools-hardened path-aware ETW lane completed shell-safe with a real I/O burst and produced real ETL/CSV outputs, but still did not capture an exact runtime read or intended-path line for AllowRemoteDASD.

evidence.title: Historical collision review for AllowRemoteDASD
evidence.summary: Earlier triage had already flagged AllowRemoteDASD as a likely removable-storage-policy collision rather than clean Session Manager I/O coverage.

decision.why: AllowRemoteDASD is backed by phase-0 baseline existence, an exact current-build ntoskrnl value-name hit, a naturally resolved current-build Ghidra route to the removable-storage policy path, and a shell-safe intended-path ETW lane that remained a clean no-hit. That is enough to keep the candidate visible, but not enough to treat the intended Session Manager I/O path as cross-layer verified.
